title: Daily Summary — 2026-05-04
body:
## Recent Activity

Synced window: 2026-05-03T12:18:03Z to 2026-05-04T12:18:03Z. Signals show 18 issues, 11 PRs, 1 discussion, and 0 commits updated.

Activity centered on release/distribution readiness: first-run onboarding, app installation bootstrap, version metadata, scheduled updates, failure reporting, project-management orchestration, route activity labels, and agent assignment. Several PRs merged, while active orchestration continued through child issues and follow-up PRs.

## Recently Active PRs

- Open: [#136 Assign accepted Sepo work to AGENT_HANDLE](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/136) adds best-effort assignment of accepted work to the configured agent handle and reached review synthesis.
- Open: [#134 Show route activity labels while agents run](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/134) implements `agent-running/<route>` labels and was reported ready to ship from child issue #25.
- Open: [#133 Add Sepo version metadata reader CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/133) adds typed `.agent/sepo-version.json` loading plus a `print-sepo-version` CLI.
- Open: [#120 Add GitHub App installation bootstrap](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/120), [#117 Report failed agent runs to Discussions](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/117), and [#116 Add scheduled Sepo update checks](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/116) remain active from the onboarding/distribution track.
- Merged: [#123 Retarget stacked PRs before branch cleanup](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/123), [#130 Add simplified Sepo version metadata foundation](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/130), [#131 Improve orchestrator handoff comments](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/131), and [#62 Add first-run onboarding setup workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/62).
- Closed without merge: [#128 Add Sepo version metadata foundation](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/128), superseded by the smaller #130 path.

## Recently Active Issues

- [#126 What's the best way to manage the tasks and project planning for this repo now?](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/126) is actively orchestrating project-management improvements; child #25 completed via PR #134 and child #135 started via PR #136.
- [#74 Create version systems](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/74) continued after the simpler #130 foundation merged; child #132 produced PR #133 for runtime metadata access.
- [#25 Display the corresponding label when running the action](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/25) produced PR #134 for route activity labels.
- [#135 Sub-orchestrator: assign-agent-handle](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/135) produced PR #136.
- [#121](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/121), [#124](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/124), [#125](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/125), [#31](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/31), and [#94](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/94) were updated but do not show completed synced implementation in this window.
- Closed in the window: [#104](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/104), [#105](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/105), [#122](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/122), [#129](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/129), and [#23](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/23).

## Recently Active Discussions

- [#118 Daily Summary — 2026-05-03](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/118) was updated with project-management summaries and managed-label application notes for active onboarding, distribution, and orchestration work.

## Follow-ups

1. Review and decide merge readiness for open PRs that reached synthesis: #133, #134, and #136.
2. Continue #126 after child #135 / PR #136 resolves, then pick the next project-management slice.
3. Revisit older open distribution PRs #116, #117, and #120 so the onboarding/update/failure-reporting track does not stall.

---
*Generated by the agent-daily-summary workflow*

- Mode: `labels applied`

### Top Triage Queue

### Notes

- Initial `gh` calls required network retry; compact metadata was used after full issue/PR histories proved very large.
- No repository mutations were performed by the agent; label application is left to the deterministic workflow step.
- Several open child issues appear implementation-complete but still open; #127 was downgraded because follow-up work has already moved to later version-metadata slices.

### Managed Label Application

- Applied 14 managed priority/effort label operation(s).
@sepo-agent /answer can u systematically review and decide what are the high priority todos for a public release of this repo?

I would **not call this ready for public release yet**. I would cut a **`v0.x` pre-release**, not `v1.0`, after the items below are done.

I reviewed discussion #137, the current repo docs/runtime, open issues/PRs, and ran `npm --prefix .agent test` on current `main`: **486 tests passed**.

**Current Architecture Snapshot**
- Sepo is already structured as a GitHub-native Actions runtime: mentions/labels route through `agent-router.yml`, then dispatch answer/implement/review/fix/orchestrate workflows. See [README.md#L65-L74](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/README.md#L65-L74).
- Runtime code is centralized in `.agent/src`, with CLIs under `.agent/src/cli` and tests under `.agent/src/__tests__`. See [overall-design.md#L66-L72](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/overall-design.md#L66-L72).
- Install docs currently say to copy `.agent/` and `.github/`, then run onboarding/memory/rubrics setup. See [install-existing-repository.md#L11-L27](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md#L11-L27).
- Version metadata now exists, but it still points at `main` with no exact source SHA: [sepo-version.json](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/sepo-version.json).

**My Priority Decision**

**P0: Must Do Before Any Public Pre-Release**
1. **Finish version/release identity work under #74.**
   - Land [#133](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/133), then make update/failure-report/install flows consume the version CLI.
   - Public installs should target a release tag, not moving `main`.
   - Publish a real GitHub tag/release, e.g. `v0.1.0` or `v0.2.0`, with `.agent/sepo-version.json` updated to exact `source_ref` + `source_sha`.

2. **Land the release-readiness PR stack that already reached SHIP.**
   - [#116](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/116) scheduled update checks: SHIP, non-draft.
   - [#120](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/120) app installation bootstrap: SHIP but draft.
   - [#133](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/133) version metadata reader: SHIP but draft.
   - [#134](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/134) running activity labels: SHIP but draft.
   - [#136](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/136) assign accepted work to agent handle: SHIP but draft.

3. **Fix and land [#117](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/117) failure reporting.**
   - This is public-release critical because installed repos need a support/bug intake path.
   - Last unresolved item I found was the best-effort auth step needing final cleanup/re-review. Also integrate version/source metadata once #133 lands.

4. **Resolve the known trigger reliability bug [#75](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/75).**
   - PR review-body commands can be cancelled by sibling inline review-comment events.
   - This is a concrete user-facing reliability problem and should not ship broadly.

5. **Do an end-to-end install smoke test in a fresh repo.**
   - Test template/fork install, app auth, provider secret setup, onboarding, memory init, rubrics init, `/answer`, `/implement`, `/review`, scheduled update, and failure report.
   - The docs describe this path at [README.md#L13-L25](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/README.md#L13-L25), but it needs a real release-candidate validation pass.

**P1: Before A Larger Public Announcement**
6. **Clarify hosted App bootstrap ownership.**
   - #120 adds the repo-side bootstrap contract, but the actual hosted webhook service is outside this repo. That external service needs to be verified before advertising “install the app and it bootstraps automatically.”

7. **Decide public access UX for external users.**
   - Current docs warn about public default access policy: [README.md#L47-L48](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/README.md#L47-L48).
   - [#31](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/31) one-shot maintainer grant is the right next UX, but I would not block a `v0.x` pre-release on it if docs clearly explain `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY`.

8. **Public docs sweep.**
   - Remove stale TODOs like scheduled/autonomous actions in [overall-design.md#L12-L13](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/overall-design.md#L12-L13).
   - Add a short release checklist, support policy, known limitations, and “update from version X to Y” docs.
   - Confirm generated files stay ignored: root `.gitignore` already ignores `.agent/dist/` and `.agent/node_modules/`.

**Recommended Execution Order**
1. Merge the SHIP PRs in dependency order: `#133`, `#116`, `#120`, `#134`, `#136`.
2. Fix/re-review/merge `#117`.
3. Implement the remaining #74 release-tag/update-metadata integration.
4. Fix #75.
5. Run fresh-repo release-candidate smoke test.
6. Cut the first public pre-release tag and GitHub Release.

Please confirm this ordering before coding; I would start with the merge/fix queue, then finish #74 as the release anchor.
